title: Valota eggersii

The species Valota eggersii is native to the Philippines and is a climbing or twining legume. This plant is likely to be classified within the subfamily Faboideae, although more specific classification may require further research. intro:
There is limited information available about Valota eggersii, it appears that it may be a lesser-known or misidentified plant species. However, I can provide information on Valota (also known as Valeriana), a plant in the Valerianaceae family.

Valeriana, or Valota, is a plant species that is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to possess numerous health benefits, including acting as a natural sedative and anxiolytic, as well as having anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana include valepotriates, valerenic acid, and valeranone, among others, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Valeriana is used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into herbal capsules and supplements.
